Professional Summary

and customer-facing roles. Strong background in loss prevention, access control, and customer service.

Trilingual (English, French, Wolof) with an active NYS security license and a proven ability to maintain safety

while delivering a positive customer experience.

coach_notes: To build a strong protective-service career, focus on developing patrol, emergency response, and incident response skills, as these are core to most security roles. Start by taking a basic first aid and CPR course, then seek on-the-job training or volunteer for patrol duties to gain practical experience. Your existing customer service and computer skills are a great foundation—leverage them to communicate effectively and document incidents, which will make you a standout candidate.

jobs.title: Asset Protection Coordinator
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Your resume skills in security presence, compliance, reporting, and documentation align directly with asset protection coordination. Experience with store policy and basic computer skills further support this role.
jobs.gaps: No specific mention of inventory auditing or incident investigation experience; may need to highlight any related tasks.

jobs.title: Asset Protection Specialist (Part Time), Henderson (Sunset)
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Part-time role matches your security presence and compliance skills. Experience with back of house and store policy is relevant for asset protection in a retail setting.
jobs.gaps: Lack of specific asset protection or loss prevention experience; may need to emphasize transferable skills from security roles.

jobs.title: Asset Protection Specialist (Part Time)
jobs.label: ready
jobs.why: Similar to Job 2, your security presence, compliance, and documentation skills are applicable. Basic computer and Microsoft skills support reporting tasks.
jobs.gaps: No direct asset protection experience; gaps in knowledge of surveillance systems or theft prevention techniques.

jobs.title: Risk Associate - Walnut Street
jobs.label: target
jobs.why: Risk associate role leverages your compliance, reporting, and documentation skills. Experience with procedures and safety is relevant.
jobs.gaps: Requires deeper risk assessment and analytical skills; no experience with risk management frameworks or data analysis tools.

jobs.title: Detention Deputy Pre-Certified
jobs.label: reach
jobs.why: Detention deputy role may value your security presence, compliance, and safety skills. Basic computer and documentation skills are transferable.
jobs.gaps: Major gaps in law enforcement training, physical fitness standards, and specific detention procedures. Pre-certification requirement indicates need for formal training.
